title: Bridge Essay: Dopamine and Addiction.
concept: Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.

**Dopamine and Addiction.** - Annu Rev Psychol (2020). DOI: 10.1146/annurev-psych-010418-103337. PMID: 31905114. Addiction is commonly identified with habitual nonmedical self-administration of drugs. It is usually defined by characteristics of intoxication or by characteristics of withdrawal symptoms. Such addictions can also be defined in terms of the brain mechanisms they activate; most addictive drugs cause elevations in extracellular levels of the neurotransmitter dopamine. Animals unable to synthesize or use dopamine lack the conditioned reflexes discussed by Pavlov or the appetitive behavior discuss
